response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine AI-native engineering experience through recent MCP and agentic workflow work at re:cinq, Staff-level titling history, and proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby. However, Python is entirely absent from his skills list and work history despite being a stated must-have, and his distributed systems depth is now six-plus years old. Stakeholder management and operational ownership (on-call, incident response) are also not demonstrated anywhere in the resume, leaving meaningful gaps against the role's non-negotiable requirements.
strengths: Direct hands-on experience with AI-native engineering: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq align with the role's baseline expectation
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and low-latency caching
Consistent Staff-level IC trajectory with technical breadth across full-stack, embedded, and tooling domains, plus public teaching and community contribution
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills section and all role descriptions — a stated must-have; no signal that this gap can be quickly closed
Cross-functional stakeholder management and product-engineering bridging are not evidenced in any role description, which is a must-have non-technical requirement
Distributed systems depth is dated (2018–2020) and there is no mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership in any position
